remarks.name: Tigera Blog (Calico / AI Security)
remarks.role: AI agent governance, security, accountability frameworks, enterprise AI deployment policy.
remarks.text: Why We Built Lynx: Bringing Control to the Age of AI Agents
remarks.content: For a decade, one idea has guided everything we’ve built at Tigera: How do you secure a dynamic system with a lot of moving parts that is changing rapidly, with a programmatic approach? Calico has... The post Why We Built Lynx: Bringing Control to the Age of AI Agents appeared first on Tigera
remarks.contentTranslation: 十年来，一个想法指导了我们在Tigera构建的一切：如何通过编程方法来确保一个具有许多快速变化的移动部件的动态系统？ Calico有...我们为什么要建造Lynx ：将控制权带入AI时代的文章首次出现在Tigera上
remarks.translation: 我们为什么要构建Lynx ：将控制权带入AI智能体时代

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Governance as acceleration: data proves it’s not a speed bump
remarks.content: For years, the enterprise AI governance conversation has been shaped by a quiet assumption: Governance is the thing that slows AI down. This may look like the review cycle that delays the launch, committee that questions the model, or even documentation requirement that sits between a wo
remarks.contentTranslation: 多年来，企业AI治理对话一直被一个安静的假设所塑造：治理是减缓AI的因素。这可能看起来像延迟发布的审核周期、质疑模型的委员会，甚至是WO之间的文档要求
remarks.translation: 治理作为加速：数据证明它不是减速带
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Generative AI governance framework: building responsible AI systems
remarks.content: Generative AI is no longer a pilot that leaders can observe from a distance. It is in contact centers, developer workflows, reporting layers, and autonomous agents acting on behalf of the business. As BARC's research on modernizing governance for the era of agentic AI shows, the stakes r
remarks.contentTranslation: 生成式AI不再是领导者可以远距离观察的试点。它存在于联系中心、开发人员工作流、报告层和代表业务行事的自主Agent中。正如BARC关于智能AI时代治理现代化的研究所表明的那样，
remarks.translation: 生成式AI治理框架：构建负责任的AI系统

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Sovereignty isn't where your AI runs. It's whether it answers to you.
remarks.content: Last week, a government order took the world's best AI model offline in hours. No one deleted it and no server failed. A single directive put the most capable model on the market out of reach for everyone not a citizen of one country.
remarks.contentTranslation: 上周，一项政府命令在数小时内让世界上最好的AI模型下线。没有人将其删除，也没有服务器发生故障。单一指令使市场上最有能力的模型对每个人不是一个国家的公民来说都是遥不可及的。
remarks.translation: 主权并不是你的AI运行的地方。问题在于它是否对你有所回应。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: What is an AI orchestration layer? Architecture, benefits, and enterprise use cases
remarks.content: AI investments are multiplying, but coordination between models, agents, and data pipelines remains an afterthought in most enterprises. The result is predictable: duplicated workflows, inconsistent outputs, ungoverned decisions, and AI initiatives that never graduate from pilot to produ
remarks.contentTranslation: AI投资正在成倍增加，但在大多数企业中，模型、Agent和数据管道之间的协调仍然是事后的想法。结果是可预测的：重复的工作流、不一致的输出、不受控制的决策以及从试点到生产的AI计划
remarks.translation: 什么是AI编排层？架构、优势和企业用例

remarks.text: AI explainability in enterprise AI: methods, tools, and why it matters for trustworthy models and agents
remarks.content: AI models, generative AI (GenAI) applications, and agents are making decisions that affect credit scores, patient care, and hiring outcomes. Enterprises deploying these systems are accumulating regulatory and reputational risk proportional to the number of decisions that cannot be explai
remarks.contentTranslation: AI模型、生成式AI(GenAI)应用程序和Agent商正在做出影响信用评分、患者护理和招聘结果的决策。部署这些系统的企业正在积累与无法解释的决策数量成比例的监管和声誉风险
remarks.translation: 企业AI中的AI可解释性：方法、工具及其对可信模型和Agent的重要性

But many enterprises still cannot route model outputs into decision

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
The past couple months we may be witnessing what the Applied AI layer will look like at scale. Despite some of the initial critique that this would just be a thin layer on the LLM, it’s turning out that actually driving agentic workflows in an enterprise is far more complex. And anywhere there’s complexity you generally gain a moat and value over time.

Here are a few of the components that appear to make up the playbook based on the examples we’re collectively seeing in coding, legal, healthcare, customer support, financial services and other fields:

* Build the features that bridge the gap between the intelligence and the workflow. Some workflows can be automated by simply going to a general purpose interface, but others need tuned interfaces and features tied to the work they’re augmenting or automating. They need features that are specific to capturing the kind of data that’s needed as context for the agent. And they need a variety of bespoke tools for the agent to use, and unique interfaces for the human-in-the-loop UX. Going far deeper than just presenting the output tokens is clearly critical, and the more depth there is here definitionally the more sustaining value.

* Act as the model router balancing frontier intelligence with cheaper models. A natural advantage that any model neutral platform has is that it can naturally (in a business model-aligned way) leverage whatever level of intelligence is necessary for the workflows they’re automating to get done. There are plenty of scenarios where you need GPT-5.5 or Fable level capability, and also lots of workloads where a more efficient closed or open weights do the trick. Only the companies that have deep evals on specific tasks across all models, and the ability business model wise to leverage them, are in a great position.

* Drive the actual implementation and change management via FDE or equivalent. A big reason the applied layer works at scale is that most enterprises need some degree of help and support with change management in implementing agents for their workflows. Data has to be cleaned up and moved to modern systems, processes have to be re-engineered and documented, workflows have to be evaled, SLAs have to get achieved, and so on. All of this is going to be unique for every type of process that gets implemented, which means the companies that have expertise in a given domain and come with all the relevant best practices will be in a strong position.

* Implement domain specific GTM that creates expertise in that field. Beyond FDEs the companies that can build sales and GTM motions aligned to their domains also have a natural advantage. Most IT and line of business leaders have too many things to do in any given day; so if you’re not on their agenda, likely someone else is. Depending on the industry, there are entirely different sets of language you use, ways of working through security and compliance, regulatory controls you have to support, industry events that companies convene at, different system integrator and consulting partners you need to work with, and so on. The more generalized this gets the less you can speak the customers language, which is where the applied layer has a leg up.

A final note. There remains a view that a lot of this is all mitigated by model intelligence alone, and the bitter lesson solves all of this in the limit. That’s possibly true, but enterprises need help changing *today*. And many aspects of how to bring intelligence to real world work don’t only depend on the axis of the pure capability of the model, so most of what you’re doing now to win ends up being important no matter how good the models get.
remarks.translation:
在过去的几个月里，我们可能会目睹应用AI层在规模上的样子。尽管最初有人批评这只是LLM的一个薄层，但事实证明，在企业中实际推动Agent工作流要复杂得多。在任何有复杂性的地方，您通常都会随着时间的推移而获得护城河和价值。
根据我们在编码、法律、医疗保健、客户支持、金融服务和其他领域共同看到的示例，以下是构成剧本的一些组成部分： *构建弥合智能和工作流之间差距的功能。有些工作流可以通过简单地转到通用界面来实现自动化，但另一些工作流则需要调整界面和与他们正在增强或自动化的工作相关的功能。
他们需要特定于捕获作为客服代表上下文所需的数据类型的功能。他们还需要各种定制工具供Agent使用，以及用于人机交互用户体验的独特界面。显然，比仅仅呈现输出代币更深层次是至关重要的，这里的深度越深，价值就越持久。*充当模型路由器，平衡前沿智能和更便宜的模型。
任何模型中立平台的一个自然优势是，它可以（以与业务模型一致的方式）自然地利用他们自动化完成工作流所需的任何智能水平。有很多场景需要GPT-5.5或寓言级功能，还有很多工作负载需要更高效的封闭式或开放式权重。
只有在所有模型中对特定任务都有深度回避的公司，以及明智地利用它们的能力的商业模式，才处于有利地位。*通过FDE或同等产品推动实际实施和变更管理。应用层大规模工作的一个重要原因是，大多数企业在实施工作流Agent的变革管理方面需要一定程度的帮助和支持。
数据必须清理并转移到现代系统，流程必须重新设计和记录，工作流必须评估， SLA必须实现，等等。所有这些对于实施的每种类型的流程都是独一无二的，这意味着在特定领域拥有专业知识并具有所有相关最佳实践的公司将处于有利地位。*实施特定领域的GTM ，在该领域创造专业知识。
除了FDE之外，能够建立与其领域一致的销售和GTM动作的公司也具有天然的优势。大多数IT和业务部门的领导者在任何一天都有太多的事情要做；因此，如果你不在他们的议程上，很可能是其他人。
根据行业的不同，您使用的语言、处理安全性和合规性的方式、您必须支持的监管控制、公司召开的行业活动、您需要与之合作的不同系统集成商和咨询合作伙伴等完全不同。越一般化，您就越不会说客户的语言，这就是应用层占优势的地方。最后提示。
仍然有一种观点认为，仅通过模型智能就可以缓解所有这些问题，而苦涩的教训可以在极限内解决所有这些问题。这可能是真的，但企业*今天*需要帮助。如何将智能带入现实工作的许多方面不仅仅取决于模型的纯粹能力轴，所以无论模型有多好，你现在所做的大部分工作最终都很重要。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.name: Zara Zhang
remarks.role: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
remarks.text:
Don't use AI for writing until you develop your own taste and voice

Using AI to write isn't inherently bad. The danger is using AI to write before you've developed your taste for what is good content. If the AI produces slop, you won't even recognize it as slop

Read a lot to figure out what good looks like. Write a lot to know what your voice sounds like. Only then, use AI to help you write, but make sure it actually sounds like you
remarks.translation: 在培养自己的品味和声音之前，不要使用AI进行写作。使用AI进行写作本质上并不是坏事。危险在于，在你培养出对优质内容的品味之前，就使用AI进行写作。如果AI产生SLOP ，你甚至不会将其识别为SLOP。阅读很多内容来弄清楚什么是好的。写下很多内容，了解您的声音听起来像什么。只有这样，才能使用AI来帮助你写作，但要确保它听起来像你
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Nikunj Kothari
remarks.role: partner fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire meter, opendoor, atlassian & others. love shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧
remarks.text:
Why am I so against tranches? 

Because it f*cks the next employee that joins. I was an early employee at four startups. My 409A would be so high. 

And why am I being subject to the ridiculous valuation that the lead preferred investor didn’t pay. 

Yes market is going to market and this will keep happening. My only agenda is to let that next employee that joins to not be duped into the FMV of their options.
remarks.translation: 为什么我如此反对分期付款？因为它会干掉下一个加入的员工。我是四家初创公司的早期员工。我的409A会很高。为什么我会受到主要优先投资者没有支付的荒谬估值的影响。是的，市场将进入市场，这将继续发生。我唯一的议程是让下一个加入的员工不要被骗入他们选择的FMV。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Nikunj Kothari
remarks.role: partner fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire meter, opendoor, atlassian & others. love shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧
remarks.text:
Spotting a tranched round is so simple in today’s market..

If someone raises xxM $ at under 10% dilution of the published valuation, you can safely bet it was a tranched round. 

This is obviously true for seed/A but sadly seeing weird tranches at the B too!
remarks.translation: 在今天的市场上，发现一轮分期融资是如此简单。如果有人以低于公布估值10%的稀释度筹集xxM $ ，你可以放心地打赌这是一轮分期融资。对于SEED/A来说，这显然是真的，但令人遗憾的是，在B区也看到了奇怪的部分！

remarks.name: Garry Tan
remarks.role: President & CEO ycombinator —Founder garryslist—Creator of GStack & GBrain—designer/engineer who helps founders—SF Dem accelerating the boom loop
remarks.text:
Rough estimate on $ productivity lost by Fable 5 ban: $12M per hour

Frontier AI-coding daily actives, mid-2026: 5M devs 
Fully-loaded cost: $90/hr 

Work routed to Fable in 48 hours: 17.8%
Fable is on average ~15% more productive 

Effective throughput loss per dev = 17.8% × 15% ≈ 2.7% of output

2.7% × $90/hr = ~$2.40/dev/hr × 5M devs = $12M per working hour
remarks.translation: Fable 5禁令对$生产力损失的粗略估计：每小时$ 1200万Frontier AI编码每日活动， 2026年中期： 500万开发人员全负荷成本： 90 $/小时48小时内路由到Fable的工作： 17.8% Fable平均生产率提高约15%每开发人员的有效吞吐量损失= 17.8% × 15% ≈ 2.7%产出2.7% × $ 90/小时= ~ $ 2.40/开发人员/小时× 500万开发人员=每工作小时$ 1200万
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Zara Zhang
remarks.role: Builder. Dangerously skips permissions. Harvard’17. GitHub: https://t.co/KCuEajezlL YouTube: https://t.co/8xzbGWtf6w
remarks.text:
The thing about vibe coded personal apps:

Building the thing takes a day. Finding out if you'll actually use it takes a week. Most of my dead projects worked fine. I just never opened them.

Most products are built for a person who doesn't exist. Someone who remembers to open the app, clicks the right button, does step 1, 2, 3 every day. Real humans are lazy & forgetful. Build for that person instead
remarks.translation: 关于vibe编码的个人应用程序：构建东西需要一天时间。确定您是否真的会使用它需要一周的时间。我的大多数失败项目都运作良好。我只是从来没有打开过它们。大多数产品都是为不存在的人打造的。记得打开应用程序的人，点击右键，每天执行第1、2、3步。真正的人类是懒惰和健忘的。改为为该人员构建
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Guillermo Rauch
remarks.role: vercel CEO
remarks.text:
React    →  https://t.co/a4QDSs9wxd
Next.js  →  https://t.co/nDDXqUmgw5

@aisdk is more relevant than ever, given the intense model competition landscape. Just today, GLM 5.2, an open model, surpassed Opus 4.8 in our Next.js Evals (https://t.co/aporqgIfIh) 🤯

But the world needs a practical solution for how to build and deploy agents. Just like React needed Next.js to solve the task of building an actual web application. And that's eve.
remarks.translation: 鉴于激烈的模型竞争格局， React → Next.js → @ aisdk比以往任何时候都更具相关性。就在今天，开放式模型GLM 5.2在我们的Next.js Evals中超过了Opus 4.8 （🤯但世界需要一个实用的解决方案来构建和部署Agent。就像React需要Next.js来解决构建实际Web应用程序的任务一样。那就是夏娃。
remarks.observation: 这条言论可作为 Agent 产品形态、协作方式或能力边界的观察入口；只代表公开观点，不作为公司事实主证据。

remarks.text: Claude Fable is relentlessly proactive
remarks.content: After two days of experience with Claude Fable 5 I think the best way to describe it is relentlessly proactive . It knows a whole lot of tricks and it will deploy pretty much any of them to get to its goal. <p

podcasts.name: Unsupervised Learning (Redpoint)
podcasts.title: Ep 89: AI Research Legend’s Honest Assessment of Where We Are
podcasts.excerpt: This episode with Lukasz Kaiser, co-author of the seminal "Attention Is All You Need" transformer paper and former researcher at both Google Brain and OpenAI, is a wide-ranging conversation about the fundamental limits of current AI architectures and whether transformers will continue to dominate or eventually give way to something new. Lukasz brings a rare dual perspective: deep belief in how far the current paradigm has taken us (he's an enthusiastic daily Codex user who's seen 10x productivity gains in his own research), while maintaining genuine intellectual humility about whether transformers can truly generalize the way humans do. The episode weaves together questions about data efficiency, the non-verifiable RL frontier, the coding agent revolution, the open vs. closed source gap, and what the next architectural leap might look like: all filtered through the lens of someone who helped bu…

podcasts.name: Unsupervised Learning (Redpoint)
podcasts.title: Ep 88: Unpacking DeepMind's Quest for SuperIntelligence with Demis Hassabis' Biographer
podcasts.excerpt: Sebastian Mallaby spent three years and 30+ hours interviewing Demis Hassabis in the back of a British pub to write The Infinity Machine, and the conversation uses that reporting to surface the most underexplored figure in AI. Demis founded the original AI lab in 2010, won a Nobel Prize, runs models that consistently top the leaderboards, and yet remains so unrecognized that Sebastian's own publisher worried no one would buy a book with his face on the cover. The throughline is a paradox: Demis tried to prevent the AI race we're now all living through, and now finds himself one of its central protagonists. He used to believe a single lab could carry the safety burden to AGI; he now sees safety as a collective action problem only governments can solve. He hedged DeepMind's research bets across every promising direction, and as a result missed the two most consumer-defining moments…
